content.problemStatement:
This morning Chef wants to jump a little. In a few minutes he will arrive at the point **0**. Then he will perform a lot of jumps in such a sequence: **1**-jump, **2**-jump, **3**-jump, **1**-jump, **2**-jump, **3**-jump, **1**-jump, and so on.

**1**-jump means that if Chef is at the point **x**, he will jump to the point **x+1**. 

**2**-jump means that if Chef is at the point **x**, he will jump to the point **x+2**. 

**3**-jump means that if Chef is at the point **x**, he will jump to the point **x+3**. 

Before the start Chef asks you: will he arrive at the point **a** after some number of jumps?
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer a.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'yes' if Chef can arrive at point a or 'no' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 0 can be represented as a sum of 0 3s.
1 can be represented as a sum of 1 1.
2 cannot be represented as a sum of 1s, 2s, and 3s.

solution.approach: The key insight is to realize that Chef can arrive at any point a if and only if a % 3 == 0 or a % 3 == 1 or (a > 1 and a % 3 == 2). This is because Chef can jump 1, 2, or 3 steps at a time, and these jumps can be combined in various ways to reach any point. The algorithm simply checks the remainder of a when divided by 3 and prints 'yes' or 'no' accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — no additional space is used.
